content.text: In a BST, most operations (search, insert, delete) run in O(height) time. For a balanced tree of n nodes, height ≈ log₂(n) — but an unbalanced tree can degenerate to height n, making every operation O(n).

A rotation is a local restructuring that moves a node closer to the root without breaking the BST property. In a splay tree, every accessed node is rolled up until it becomes the root — frequently accessed nodes stay near the top. Balanced nodes (height difference ≤ 1) are shown in
